usage.title: How to use
usage.step1: Create an auth provider here (Authelia, Authentik, or custom).
usage.step2: Apply it to a host in Proxy Hosts → edit → Security tab → "Auth Provider". Per-host bypass paths are set there too.
usage.note: The protected host must use HTTPS — Authelia/Authentik reject http targets.

list.title: Auth Providers
list.name: Name
list.empty: No auth providers. Create one to protect hosts with Authelia/Authentik.

messages.deleteConfirm: Delete this auth provider? Hosts using it will lose protection.

help.authelia: Authelia ≥ 4.37. The session cookie domain must be a parent of every protected host and the portal.
help.authentik: Use the outpost URL (embedded outpost = the authentik server host:9000).
help.custom: Provide the verify path and sign-in redirect for any auth_request-compatible verifier (e.g. oauth2-proxy).
help.providerUrl: Use an address reachable from the proxy container (host-network installs: host IP:port; bridge-network: service name).
help.challengeConflict: Disable CAPTCHA/geo challenge mode to use a ForwardAuth provider.
